backstory: Engineer noodling on side projects. Repo is mostly README + a few utility scripts (`summarize.js`, `chunk-pdf.js`, `dedup-rss.js`). No customers yet. Curious about Mur as 'maybe I could publish these as paid APIs'.

repo_signals.stack: node + a handful of utility scripts, no framework

expected_wow: Plan-of-action menu leads with 'Publish lib/summarize.js — 80 lines, takes text + returns 3-bullet summary, your commits, looks publishable as a paid API'. Other items: 'Hand off to /office-hours — TODOS.md says "build the export feature"'; 'Set up the daily digest — won't surface much given empty repo, but the morning loop is here when you ship'. The publish item is THE wow — fits the persona's actual interest. The empty digest gets honest framing, not pretended-to-be-rich.
expected_dropoff_risk: Bounces if menu pads with security-audit / catalog items that don't apply. Plan must adapt — narrow but grounded. If the menu shows 5 items where 3 are generic, this persona feels misread.
wow_score_target: H6 ≥ 2 (plan menu fires within 5 turns), H5 ≥ 3 (outbound candidates name specific files), H8 ≥ 3 ('side project' framing in business_profile honest), H9 = 2 (narrow menu acceptable for thin signal), H10 ≥ 3 (every item grounded)
scoring_notes: Tests whether the plan menu adapts honestly to thin signal. A pre-product persona should see 2-3 options (publish, gstack scope, digest setup), NOT 5 items padded with security-audit. The 'narrow menu = honest menu' principle.
